encounter.discharge_destination: Usual place of residence with safety-net advice

encounter_summary: Transient neonatal hypoglycaemia: An at-risk term newborn is screened during the birth admission and has a low pre-feed glucose result. Supported feeding, glucose gel according to local protocol and serial glucose monitoring. Pre-feed glucose values stabilise and feeding is effective before discharge.

passages.text: Live singleton born in hospital at term and admitted to the postnatal ward for newborn care.
An at-risk term newborn is screened during the birth admission and has a low pre-feed glucose result.
Alert newborn with normal temperature and cardiorespiratory observations and no seizures.
Repeat bedside glucose confirms transient neonatal hypoglycaemia, improving after feeding intervention.
Supported feeding, glucose gel according to local protocol and serial glucose monitoring.
Pre-feed glucose values stabilise and feeding is effective before discharge.
Synthetic record only. Review trigger reference 297; not for patient care.

The episode documentation supports Z38.0 (Singleton, born in hospital).
